name: Reduced-Fat Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Coat three 9-inch round layer-cake pans with non-stick cooking spray.
3. Line the bottoms of the pans with wax paper and set aside.
4. In a pie plate, spread walnuts and bake for 8-10 minutes at 350 degrees F,
or until fragrant. Let cool; chop coarsely.
5. Toasting the walnuts brings out their nutty flavor, requiring fewer
ingredients than traditional recipes.
6. In a medium-sized bowl, stir together flour, cinnamon, baking powder,
baking soda, and salt.
7. In a large bowl, whisk eggs and egg whites. Add sugar, prune puree, and oil;
whisk until smooth.
8. Add the flour mixture to the egg mixture and stir with a wooden spoon
until blended. Stir in carrots, pineapple, and toasted walnuts.
9. Divide the batter among the prepared pans and bake for 30-35 minutes at
350 degrees F, or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Let cool for 5 minutes in the pan on a rack. Loosen edges and invert cakes
onto racks. Peel off paper and let cool completely.
11. To make prune puree: In a food processor, combine 6 ounces (1 cup) pitted
prunes with 6 tablespoons hot water; process until smooth. Makes 1 cup.
- One cup of prune puree contains 407 calories and 1 gram of fat;
a cup of butter contains 1,600 calories and 182 grams of fat;
and a cup of oil contains 1,944 calories and 218 grams of fat.
12. To assemble the cake: Place one cake layer on a serving plate. Spread
about 1/2 cup frosting over it. Top with another cake layer and spread
another scant 1/2 cup frosting. Place the third layer on top and spread
with remaining frosting. Serves 16.
13. Calories: 310 per serving; Protein: 6 grams; Fat: 10 grams;
Carbohydrate: 52 grams; Sodium: 392 milligrams; Cholesterol: 34 milligrams.
